name: Sopeña King's Trail
name_original: Eskutxi  y Ungino — circular por Sierra Salvada
description: Hike the moderate 9.0-mile Eskutxi and Ungino circular trail in Sierra Salvada, featuring panoramic views and the Ojo de Ungino rock arch.
region_intro: Bus station hiking trails in Gorobel Mendilerroa Bbe/Zepa Sierra Sálvada traverse a landscape characterized by impressive limestone crags, rocky ledges, and a natural wall that can drop over 600 meters. The region features an extensive paramera with calcicolous pastures, low scrub, and interspersed beech and pine groves. This karst formation includes high meadows, dolines, and sinkholes, contributing to a diverse terrain for hikers. Notable natural features include the Nervión Waterfall and prominent peaks like Eskutxi and Tologorri. Best bus station hiking trails in Gorobel Mendilerroa Bbe/Zepa Sierra Sálvada The most popular bus station hiking route is Urieta and Moscadero Loop from Angulo, a difficult 12.5 miles (20.1 km) trail that takes 7 hours 6 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is View from Eskorita – ErmitaEtxaurren loop from Arespalditza / Respaldiza, a moderate 4.8 miles (7.7 km) path. This trail offers views from Eskorita and passes by ErmitaEtxaurren. Local hikers also love the View of Amurrio – Bandits' Trail loop from Amurrio, an 8.3 miles (13.3 km) trail leading through the Amurrio area, often completed in about 4 hours 5 minutes. Hiking in Gorobel Mendilerroa Bbe/Zepa Sierra Sálvada is defined by rugged limestone terrain, dramatic waterfalls, and extensive beech forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to challenging ascents. The routes in Gorobel Mendilerroa Bbe/Zepa Sierra Sálvada are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 570 reviews. More than 4800 hikers have used komoot to explore Gorobel Mendilerroa Bbe/Zepa Sierra Sálvada's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many bus station hiking trails are available in Gorobel Mendilerroa Bbe/Zepa Sierra Sálvada?
A: There are 12 hiking trails accessible directly from bus stations in Gorobel Mendilerroa Bbe/Zepa Sierra Sálvada, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: Are there easy hiking options from bus stations for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are 4 easy routes perfect for beginners or families. A great example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40315848" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amurrio – View of Amurrio loop from Amurrio&lt;/a&gt;, which is relatively flat and offers pleasant views without significant elevation gain.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes starting from bus stations?
A: Yes, many of the trails accessible from bus stations are circular, allowing you to return to your starting point without retracing your steps. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31573538" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Eskorita – ErmitaEtxaurren loop from Arespalditza / Respaldiza&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate circular hike.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on these bus-accessible trails?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can expect dramatic limestone crags, extensive beech forests, and impressive viewpoints. Some trails might even offer glimpses of the spectacular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2824175" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nervión Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, especially after heavy rains, or lead to prominent peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3079416" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Txarlazo&lt;/a&gt; with its panoramic views.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to the Nervión Waterfall from a bus station?
A: While direct bus routes to the waterfall's immediate vicinity might be limited, some trails from nearby towns can connect you to the wider area. The region around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2824175" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nervión Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a key highlight, and routes in the area often provide access to viewpoints or the Delika Gorge below it.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest along the bus station hiking trails?
A: Beyond natural features, you might encounter historical and cultural points. Some routes offer views of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3079407" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Fraile&lt;/a&gt;, or lead to summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3361908" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tologorri&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features monuments and even Civil War emplacements, providing a glimpse into the region's past.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike these bus-accessible trails?
A: Spring and autumn are generally ideal. Spring brings lush greenery and wildflowers, while autumn transforms the beech forests into vibrant displays of color. After heavy rains or during thaw, the Nervión Waterfall is at its most impressive. Summers can be warm, and winters may present snow and ice at higher elevations, requiring appropriate gear.

Q: Are the bus station hiking trails suitable for dog walkers?
A: Many trails in the Gorobel Mendilerroa Bbe/Zepa Sierra Sálvada region are dog-friendly, but it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or protected wildlife. Ensure you carry enough water for both yourself and your pet, as water sources might be scarce on some routes.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Gorobel Mendilerroa Bbe/Zepa Sierra Sálvada?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars. Reviewers often praise the dramatic landscapes, the tranquility of the beech forests, and the rewarding panoramic views from peaks like Txarlazo. The diverse terrain, from easy loops to challenging ascents, is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any challenging routes accessible from bus stations for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, there are 2 difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047997383" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urieta and Moscadero Loop from Angulo&lt;/a&gt;, for example, involves significant elevation gain and covers over 20 kilometers, offering a strenuous but rewarding experience.

Q: Can I combine hiking with birdwatching on these trails?
A: Absolutely. Gorobel Mendilerroa Bbe/Zepa Sierra Sálvada is a Special Protection Area (SPA) for birds, making it an excellent spot for birdwatching. Keep an eye out for griffon vultures, Egyptian vultures, peregrine falcons, and even golden eagles, especially in the higher, rocky areas.

Q: Are there any moderate difficulty trails from bus stations that offer good views?
A: Yes, there are 6 moderate routes that provide excellent views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38181055" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Amurrio – Bandits' Trail loop from Amurrio&lt;/a&gt; is a great option, offering scenic vistas and a good workout without being overly strenuous.
tips: One of the most spectacular views of Sierra Salvada.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this hike?
faqs.answer: The full circular route is approximately 14.5 kilometers long and generally takes around 4 hours and 46 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your pace, stops for views, and physical condition.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this circular trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite diverse. You'll encounter well-maintained tracks, some cemented or gravel, especially in initial sections. However, it quickly transitions to narrower, steeper paths towards the peaks. A significant portion, particularly around Eskutxi and Ungino, involves walking over lapiaz, which is uneven karstic limestone pavement requiring careful footing. Some descents, like from Portillo de Atatxa, can be steep and potentially slippery if wet.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: This trail offers several notable highlights. You'll pass by the West Ring viewpoint, the East Aro summit, and the Eskutxi summit, which offers unparalleled panoramic views. Don't miss the Ungino Cave and the iconic Ungino Summit and Rock Arch, also known as the 'Ojo de Ungino'.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this trail?
faqs.answer: Common starting points for this circular trail include the villages of Salmantón or Madaria. You'll typically find parking available in or near these villages, which serve as convenient access points to the Sierra Salvada.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally welcome on trails in Sierra Salvada, it's important to be aware of the challenging terrain, especially the lapiaz sections and steep descents, which might be difficult for some dogs. Always keep your dog under control, preferably on a leash, to protect wildlife and ensure their safety on exposed sections.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is best enjoyed in dry conditions due to the challenging terrain, particularly the steep descents and karstic limestone. Spring and autumn often offer pleasant temperatures and clear views. Summer can be good, but be prepared for heat. Winter can bring snow and ice, significantly increasing the difficulty and requiring specialized gear.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for hiking in Sierra Salvada?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail or access the Sierra Salvada mountain range. However, always respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as moderate in difficulty, but some sections can be quite demanding. It requires a good physical condition and steady footing, especially when navigating the uneven lapiaz and steep descents. While not for absolute beginners, hikers with some experience and good fitness should find it manageable.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route utilizes sections of other paths. You will find yourself on parts of the "Sopeñako errege bidea" and briefly on the "Acceso vía ferrata" and "Final via ferrata" trails.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or tips for hiking this route?
faqs.answer: Be particularly cautious on the descent from Portillo de Atatxa, as it is known to be very steep and can be challenging, especially if the ground is wet. The extensive lapiaz (karstic limestone pavement) around Eskutxi and Ungino requires careful attention to foot placement. Dry conditions are ideal for this hike. Always carry enough water, as sources on the trail are limited.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Public transport options directly to the common starting points like Salmantón or Madaria can be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for services to nearby towns such as Orduña or Artziniega, and then arrange for a taxi or consider a longer walk to the trailhead.
